_note: Marion's knowledge manifest. Marion reads this FIRST, then loads every file listed under 'manufacturers' and merges their products into one catalog. To add a manufacturer: drop its file in /knowledge/manufacturers/ and add a line here. To temporarily disable one: set active=false. Bump 'knowledge_version' whenever you publish a change.

oem_parts.source: live website (sealsonline.com flabed John Deere parts)
oem_parts.note: OEM part-number -> Fluidseal part-number layer (part->part, not profile->profile). 82 real Fluidseal stock parts across 12 part types with verified leaf URLs. Convention: RJD- + the John Deere reference number (exception: filler-ring RJD-TD<diameter> internal series). Carries a DATA_INTEGRITY_FLAG about the Serge estimate's RJD-TD187116 vs. the live RJD-T<number> convention — needs David's confirmation.

oem_parts_note: oem-parts/ holds OEM brands (John Deere, and future Caterpillar/Hitachi/etc.) whose relationship to Fluidseal is part-number -> part-number plus that part's own dimensional detail, NOT a seal-profile bridge. These are NOT merged into the seal-profile catalog; Marion and quote.html consult them to resolve a customer's OEM part reference to a confirmed Fluidseal part number + leaf URL.
crossref_note: parent-profiles.json is the canonical join layer: every vendor/manufacturer profile (Fluidseal/SSG, Fluidseal Xpress, DMH, Parker, Hallite, Freudenberg, Maxspare) maps to a parent_profile, which resolves to the Fluidseal/SSG equivalent. crossref.json is the older category-bridge for Hallite/Clipper and is superseded by parent-profiles for any part with an explicit parent.
markets_note: markets/ holds application & market-context knowledge (NOT profile/part-number data). markets.json maps 21 industries to their operating drivers, seal types, and a routing hint to Fluidseal product-group families; materials-context.json holds material operating envelopes referenced by market entries. Sourced from Freudenberg market/application brochures (raw PDFs archived in markets/freudenberg-brochures/ with _manifest.json). Use for 'where do seals go / why' reasoning; not a verified equivalent mapping.

manufacturers.name: Fluidseal
manufacturers.note: Fluidseal's own normalized profile namespace — the translation layer other manufacturers map into. Each profile carries a verified sealsonline.com leaf URL.

manufacturers.name: Clipper
manufacturers.note: Detailed-data subset of Clipper oil seals (full spec/dimensional data). The complete Parker-published Clipper profile index is in parker-rotary.json under the 'Clipper Oil Seals & Parker Oil Seals' family.

manufacturers.name: Parker
manufacturers.note: Fluid Power Seal Design Guide EPS 5370 (Engineered Polymer Systems Division). 64 profiles across 8 groups extracted from the catalog profile-selector tables. parent_profile and fluidseal_equivalent are null pending David's Parker comparable list; once supplied, wire these into parent-profiles.json (Parker column currently scaffolded/empty).

manufacturers.name: Parker Rotary
manufacturers.note: Rotary Seal Design Guide EPS 5350. 217 profiles across 4 families (160 Clipper/Parker Oil Seals, 40 FlexiSeal PTFE, 14 ProTech Bearing Isolators, 3 Shaft Sleeves). Stored as profiles_by_family code lists; expand to per-profile objects when comparable mappings are added. Distinct from parker.json (reciprocating). SB and SM appear in two families as distinct profiles — disambiguate by family.

manufacturers.name: Freudenberg
manufacturers.note: Freudenberg Sealing Technologies (FST) — Merkel / Simmerring brands. v1.1: 157 profiles across 17 categories from the FST Product Overview 2019. v1.0 captured 118; v1.1 recovered 39 additional profiles previously dropped to PDF column-reflow ambiguity (re-parsed by directed page-region matching and verified against page layout). Primary (leftmost) spec column captured per profile; multi-variant material/limit columns not yet split out. parent_profile and fluidseal_equivalent are null pending David's Freudenberg comparable list (Freudenberg column in parent-profiles.json currently scaffolded/empty). Simrit/Freudenberg OEM crossref is also tracked via anyseals.json. Remaining un-extracted FST items are the diaphragm/accumulator/boots/special-products families (pp. 85-96) which are descriptive product lines without pressure/temp/speed spec tables.
